Internal Launches
!
Now, I'm going to talk briefly about the Internal Launch. In those first two modules we
went through we talked about the real and core strategies and tactics. Then, we just

covered the Seed Launch and the Quick Launch. The Seed Launch is great for starting
out if you have no list, no product. You want to create a product. Or, if you've got a list
but you got a new idea for product and you want to create that product and get paid
before you create that product.
Quick Launch is great for when you already have a business and list, but you want a
quick payday. The Internal Launch is sort of our next step. Now, if you're just a
beginner then the Internal Launch isn't necessarily going to apply to you right now. I
just want you to absorb this. I'm just going to go over briefly here and I want you to
absorb this and let this information just wash over your business. This is going to pay
off big time after you do your Seed Launch.
In the next module, we're going to go deep into creating the Pre-prelaunch and into
creating your Pre-Launch. That's when we're going to really take that material and put
it into your Internal Launch. This is just a brief introduction, a bit of a tease I guess
about the Internal Launch.
Internal Launches are launches to just your list, where you have no affiliates
or joint venture partners.
I often get questions around the difference between affiliates and joint venture
partners, and in reality, there isn't very much difference. In terms of the way you're
tracking in your commissions or anything, impractical terms there's no difference
between affiliate and a joint venture partner.
In my mind, a JV partner is someone that I've got a warmer closer relationship with.
The way I look it, I've been out to dinner with all of them and I have their cellphone
number.
In any case, with the Internal Launch, you're not using affiliates or joint venture
partners. You're doing it just to your list. Now, there are some advantages to that and
I'll get to the just a second. Basically, this is the classic Sideways Sales Letter launch
where you have your three pieces of Pre-Launch content, usually three videos that lead
into a sales letter or a sales video.
With the Internal Launch, you're usually going to get bigger results than a Seed Launch
or Quick Launch. It's more work than a Quick Launch, it's more work than a Seed
Launch, but you're generally going to get bigger results. This is the next step up. If you
go back to module one, in that circle of awesome, I talked about how it basically goes
from a Seed Launch, to an Internal Launch, to a JV Launch. You don't have to go in that
order but that's the classic circle of awesome.
Now, this is the Internal Launch is a lot simpler than a big JV Launch. That's where the
Internal Launch fits. It fits bigger results than the Seed Launch but simpler than the JV
Launch. The way I look at this you're going to take that side way sales or you're going
to create that Sideways Sales Letter. Then, do your Internal Launch and then usually,
you could take that Sideways Sales Letter, take that same launch, the same asset that

you create as part of that launch and re-use it in a JV Launch. That's why it fits in
between that Seed Launch and the JV Launch.
A lot of times, people say they see the glory of the JV Launch. That there are more
dollars in the JV Launch, and they just want to go for it right away. I would generally
warn against that. But there are times when some people do that, they skip right to the
JV Launch. There was one time when where I skipped right to the JV Launch.
Let's just say your Internal Launch is X amount of work. Then, to do at JV Launch, it
takes 2X. It's twice as much work to do the JV Launch because now, you have to
manage JV partners. That ends up being a lot of work. It just is. Even though you're re-
using your Sideways Sales Letter from your Internal Launch, when you do that JV
Launch, it's a bunch of work.
The Internal Launch is the real bread and butter in the PLF world. I love them because
there is so much use in JV Launches. Really, the majority of launches out there that PLF
Owners are doing tend to be this Internal Launches. I consider them very elegant
Launches.
The reason that they're elegant is you do have maximum control. They happen on your
time frame. For instance, let's say you have that classic Sideways Sales Letter where
you have three videos. Say you're at least video one, day one of your Pre-Launch and
video two and day four of your Pre-Launch. I'm going to get into all this later on in the
next module. All the timing and stuff.
Let's just say day one, four and seven are the days you release your videos. Day one,
you release video one, day four you release video two and day seven, you release video
three. In an Internal Launch, that means on day one, you're mailing your list. Then, on
day four, about PLC one. Then, on day four, you're mailing your list about PLC two. On
day seven, you're mailing your list about PLC three and you're setting up for that sales
video. You controlling exactly when those emails go out. It's real nice that way.
Now, if you have JV Launch, you don't know exactly when your partners going to mail.
They might wait until day three to mail video one. They might wait until day seven to
mail video two. In fact, they might wait until day eight to mail video one. You just never
know exactly when your partners are going to mail. You're going to have different
partners mailing on different videos. You just lose some of the control of the messaging
and the timing when you go to the JV Launch.
Now, the benefit of that is that with a JV Launch you're going to have a lot traffic, and it
tends to be a much bigger launch. That's why I've got this certain fondness for these
Internal Launches. They really are elegant. You really do control the messaging much
more all the way through the launch so I really like them.
But also, they happen in your time frame. There's a couple of things about that. First of
all, what I'm just talking about is the exact timing of what gets mailed. That's a big
deal. Then another big deal is, once you start to do JV Launches, you have to plan your

planning horizon extends out quite a bit further. Because you have to plan it for time
when your partners have space in their publication calendar. You have to warn them

If you want to do an Internal Launch next Monday and it's Thursday, you can knock out
those, the PLC one. By Monday, you could do it. You could just spend the weekend
knocking out PLC one and go ahead and send an email on Monday. Then, you'll be
scrambled and do PLC two and PLC three but you could do it. With that JV Launch, you
can't just like on Thursday, mail your partners and say, "Hey, I'm doing JV Launch on

to have room in their calendar in time to put the promotion together means they are

You have to plan on a much longer time frame. For example, in my business, I plan out
my JV Launches, we're actually planning the full year in advance. It's crazy but for a
November Launch, we're starting to plan that October, November, December the year
before. We're locking in dates. We start telling our partners six months, maybe the nine
months in advance we're telling our partners. It’s great to have long-term planning, it
locks you into those dates. Now, my partners are depending on me to mail in that time
frame.
Then, if something else comes up, if my schedule conflicts with it, then I've got a real
problem. For instance, I've got a big life outside of my business. One of the things I
love to do is take wilderness river or backpacking trips. One of my favorite things is to
take my boat down to the Colorado river through the Grand Canyon. That trip is a trip
of a lifetime. It takes two or three weeks. I've done it several times. When I do it, I
actually take my own boat, no guides, whatsoever. Just my boat. I go with some friends
and we have to get a permit. Then, once we get the permit, we're putting the trip
together on our own.
The thing is, is like, if I've got a Launch plan for September and then a permit becomes

Because I've got my JV Launch plan. You do lose a lot of flexibility when you go to a JV

-- 6 of 57 --

If for instance, you're selling a real high end product with a high end price with
thousand dollars or two thousand dollars or even more. Then smaller list if you have
three hundred people and they're engaged, you're probably going to be able to
generate enough sales or it's going to do pretty well.
If you're selling a product for $19 or $47 and your list of 300 people. Then you just
might, your conversions, you're just not going to convert enough people to make that
launch really attractive. Now, the good news is if you're on a load, a type of niche
market where there's lower prices. Then, it's usually easier to build a bigger list because
it's a bigger market. That's the way it is. Smaller markets generally have higher prices.
Bigger markets tend to have lower prices.
For example, if you're doing a product in time management, there’s a lot of people that
are interested in that. It's going to be easier to build a bigger list but it's probably going
to have a lower price point vs. if you had a product that was time management for
plastic surgeons. Because then you're going to have a smaller market but it's probably
going to be a higher price project. Even if the stuff in the product is fairly similar.
The only thing is, if people ask about timing how long to wait between a Seed Launch
and an Internal Launch. Typically, it's going to be about three months. Because say you
do a Seed Launch and you have promised five webinars or tele-seminars or webcast or
hangouts and you do it like let's say, one a week for five weeks. Then you do another
bonus one. It's taking you six weeks to deliver it. Then maybe it's going to take you a
few weeks to get the transcripts done and put it together into a real product. In the
end, you're probably looking at three months.
The real answer is, once you start to really do these launches and get a feel for them,
then you'll be able to get a feel for your list and when they're ready for it. You'll literally
be able to sense just the interaction with the list whether you're ready. Another
question is often, can you skip the Seed Launch and just go straight to the Internal
Launch? The answer is absolutely. If you have your product done already and you have
your bigger list. Then, just forget the Seed Launch and go straight to the Internal
Launch and you get bigger results.
The Seed Launch really is about creating the product. It's about getting started it from
scratch. If you've already got a business, you're already rolling then just go straight to
the Internal Launch. Finally, a lot of times, people are like, "Hey, I've got a cold list. Can
I do an Internal Launch to a cold list?" Go back to module one. I had a video in
warming a cold list. If you do that and you warm your list, then you can absolutely do
an Internal Launch for your cold list.
That's it for the Internal Launch. Like I said, we're going to go deep into the pre Pre-
Launch and the Pre-Launch in the next module. I just wanted to give you a quick
introduction for the Internal Launch.

Timelines for Your Internal Launch
!
Okay, so now I want to talk about the timing of the pre-launch.
!
Lots and lots of questions about this, I always get lots of questions. It’s only natural
because you see the sequences and want to know how they all fit together.
!
To be clear, this is not to scale. I’m going to split it. It’s basically three main periods
here.
!
In the middle we have the pre-launch. You’ve heard me talk about pre-launch. That’s
the sideways sales letter where you release your three pieces of pre-launch content,
you’ve got people all excited about your launch. That’s the pre-launch.
!
Then we come into the open cart. That’s where you actually open up and start taking
orders, that's the open cart.
!
We have a pre-launch period and open cart period.
!
The one you haven’t heard me talk too much about is what we call the pre-prelaunch.
!
The pre-prelaunch could be the longest of all these, so the pre-pre-launch can last
anywhere from 1 to 4 weeks long.
!
They’re usually about 2 weeks long for me. They’re a lot less defined than these 2
periods. I’m actually going to be talking about the pre-prelaunch in this module and
walking you through some examples.
!
A pre-prelaunch can be very, very cool. It’s something you don’t have to do, but once
you start using some type of a pre-prelaunch then you’ll realize how cool they are and
then you’ll start to think about them more often.
!
Then the pre-launch period is typically 9 to 12 days long in most launches. It could be
longer, could be shorter.
!
Now typically bigger-ticket items, or if you come out with a brand new flagship product,
a really big important launch for you, it’s generally going to be longer.
!
If it’s more of a quicker, a lower-priced product for you, then it could be shorter. Of
course, pricing is completely relative to whatever market you’re in.
!
If you’re teaching Fortune 500 executives how to present themselves on stage, okay,
that’s probably a really high-ticket item, high-dollar item. If you’re teaching people how
to decorate cakes, probably not going to be as high-priced.
When I’m talking about whether something’s high-priced or low-priced, it’s relative to
your market. I don’t know what cake decorating products go for, but maybe at the low
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 8

end they’re $10, at the high end maybe they’re $50.
The thing is, with those executives, teaching those Fortune 500 executives how to
present, you’re probably talking more like $2000 might be the low end and $10,000
might be the high end. Again, when I’m talking about higher-ticket items and lower-
ticket items, it’s relative to your market.
!
You never have to apologize to me if you have a lower-end market. I’ve got plenty of
PLF owners who sell lots and lots of products that are not $1000,$2000 or even $5000.
They might be selling $10 products or $100 products and selling tons of them and
they’ve got a great business. Don’t ever apologize to me or anyone else about your
pricing. The key is, is I think your using these type of strategies, you should be at the
high end of whatever your market is so it’s completely relative to what other people are
selling in your market.
!
In any case, typically a higher-ticket item’s going to have a longer pre-launch and a
lower-ticket item’s going to have a shorter pre-launch. You need a lot less copy, a lot
less marketing to sell a gallon of milk compared to selling a Lamborghini, right? It just
makes sense. A higher-ticket sale is a more involved sale, generally. It needs more
education and just more communication.
!
Most pre-launch periods, 9 to 12 days and then the open cart period is
typically 5 to 7 days.
!
Now you can go shorter. I’ve had open cart periods for my launches that were like a
day-and-a-half, okay? The thing is, if you’re going to go really short, probably not a
good thing to do on your very first launch because if something isn't perfect, it doesn’t
give you time to recover. A longer cart period gives you more time just to tweak your
message and recover from any mistakes you make during your open cart.
!
Remember there’s that product launch parachute. The product launch parachute
means that there’s lots and lots of interactions throughout this entire process so if
something isn’t perfect, you have time to recover.
!
You’re not depending on one email or one video or one anything. Those really short
open-cart periods, they’re a little on the dangerous side and when I did them, well, I’m
a trained professional. I’ve done dozens and dozens and dozens of these launches so I
felt comfortable doing it.
!
Now on the other hand, go beyond 7 days and it will often turn into a beg-a-thon,
because when your cart is open, you’re going to want to be emailing your launch list
every single day.
Frankly, after 7 days you start to run out of things to say for most launches. Later on I’ll
get into what you actually do during that open cart period.
!

I think for most people, 5 to 7 days is that golden period. Let’s just blow this out a little
bit...
!
Typically on day 1 of your prelaunch period is your first piece of PLC. This is just classic.
All this stuff’s very, very flexible and you can change stuff around.
!
PLC number 2 would typically be on day 4. You release your pre-launch content then on
day 1. Then day 2, day 3, you let people digest it. Then day 4 will be your second piece
of pre-launch content, and then on day 8, will be PLC number 3.
!
Then you’ll open cart on day 10. This is day 10 and you’re going to open. This can vary
quite a bit.
!
You can go day 1, you could go day 3, you could go day 7, you could go day 9, no
problem, but that’s typically how it will go.
!
The key here is you want to give your people a little bit of time to digest each piece of
content as you move forward. That’s just general timing, and then of course from the
open cart you go forward 5 days.
!
From the start of pre-launch to when you close cart will typically be anywhere from 14
to 19 days. In other words, if you wanted the short end here, you want 9 days pre-
launch and 5 days open cart, that would give you 14 days from the start of pre-launch
to the end, to the closing cart.
!
If you wanted a 12-day pre-launch and you kept the cart open for 7 days, that’s 19
days from the start of pre-launch to the end of the launch when you close the cart.
!
When I say close cart, that’s one little asterisk. We’ll get to this in more detail later on,
but when I say close cart, that means you’re closing your launch offer. At the end of
every launch, there has to be some negative consequence if people don’t buy from you.
!
One silly way I put it is something bad has to happen if they don’t take action, but that
doesn’t mean you have to pull the product off the market.
!
Lots of times I do pull the product off the market because of the nature of the way I
teach Product Launch Formula. Just because I do that doesn’t mean you have to do it.
!
There’s lots of other ways to basically put that definitive end on your launch...
! • 	you can close the cart, meaning that people can’t buy from you
• 	you can raise the price
• 	bonuses can go away
!

Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 1
!
The pre-prelaunch or as we’d like say, call it the pre-pre and as the name implies, this
happens before your pre-launch even starts.
!
This is really under the radar before anyone even knows what’s going on. 	Generally,
people don’t have a hint what you’re really leading into. 	This is the pre-pre, this is
where you really identify objections and gather testimonials.
Your going to want to use the pre-prelaunch to warm your list. 	You might even start
attracting partners and really, it’s about starting to build buzz ahead of the launch. 	This
is where you lay the groundwork for your launch.
!
We usually start off with what I call “the shot across the bow.” 	This is an old naval
term and it would be like, if one navy ship is chasing down another ship or maybe the
coastguard’s ship is chasing down a boat, they would often fire a shot in front of the
other boat, land the shot in the water just to warn them that someone is coming so I
like to call this “the shot across the bow.” 	The essence of the pre-pre is that you’re
letting your market know that something is coming but you’re not actually trying to tell
them.
!
In fact, a big part of message is that the product is not quite ready yet. 	If your product
isn't even ready, you can’t actually be selling it yet, right? 	So you remember that BS
Detector that we talked about in the sideways sales letter? The BS Detector, there’s that
red zone, you want to avoid that red zone and when in you’re in the pre-pre, you’ll see
that it’s very easy to avoid that red zone in the pre-pre. 	Be sure you’re not actually
even selling anything to anyone and when you start off with that BS Detector in the
safe zone, it’s easier to keep it in that safe zone.
!
When there’s no hint of a pitch and you tell people that something is coming and you’re
really excited about it, it’s like you might come out and say, “Hey, we’ve been working
on this thing for years. 	We’re so excited. I can’t wait to bring it to you. 	Can you just
help us out by filling out this survey before we release this?”
!

That is a lot friendlier than something like, “Hey, we’ve been working on this thing for

Buzz travels most easily through channels that have trust and authenticity. They’re not

The people in these inner rings are the people that, not only are most interested in

Now, one of the things is, people like to support what they help create. 	This is a key

People like to support that what they help create.
!
So if they give you some feedback, if I go out to my people, to my followers and say,

!
They are now part of the creation and people like to support that which they helped

!

together, they love to tell others about it.
!
They love having the feeling that they have some insider knowledge... but having

So people are going to feel great about connecting the breadcrumbs together and now,
they’ve got a secret and then they want to go out and share it.
!
If you can string these breadcrumbs together and create a little mystery, it can go a

and lots and lots of other people. They have big followings in social media.

A 	lot 	of 	people 	that 	you 	see 	publishing 	very 	actively 	in 	social 	media, 	they 	are

!

!

!
It’s sort of funny. Back then I was the poster child for demanding absolute perfection.
In any case, “This course will entirely focus on support and resistance. It includes two
printed manuals, eight audio CDs and one computer-based video tutorial CD. It’s going
to be a complete brain dump of everything that Mike and I know about support and
resistance.”
!
Nowadays I think I would do the survey earlier in the process. I actually had the whole
thing mostly done. Nowadays I would not do that. I would do this earlier in the process.
!
I’m a lot smarter now than I was back then. In any case, “We’re going to cover all the
ways we use to generate our support and resistance zones.” These two paragraphs sort
of are doing a little bit of pre-selling. Down here, it’s “However, we need your help
before we finalize everything and send it to the printer. We need to make sure we’ve
considered everything. This is where you come in. Please take a few minutes to answer
this super short survey. There’s only two questions we need you to answer.”
!
Then just ask them, “What are your top two questions about support and resistance
that you absolutely need, that we need to answer in this course?” So we ask them for
their top question, and then we’re asking, “What type of computer do you use?” One of
the reasons we were working with the formatting of the disks and we wanted to make
sure 	that 	we 	covered 	everything 	we 	needed 	in 	terms 	of 	the 	different 	types 	of
computers.
!
Then after they got that, they would fill this in. They would hit this Next button, and
this is the next screen that would come up. It says, “Thanks for your response. That’s
what we needed. If you want us to contact you as soon as the support and resistance
course is ready, just leave your name and email address below. We will not share your
email address with anyone for any reason.” Now we’re just hitting them for their contact
information. Here’s where some of the real magic happened. If they actually went
ahead and filled in their information, then they got this third screen.
!
This third screen, “Okay, we got it. Thanks. We’ll be in touch.”
Think about the psychology here. They already went through the first screen that asked
them what their top question was. Then we came back and we asked them for their
contact information. At this point we’ve only got people looking at this that are pretty
darn 	interested. 	They 	fill 	out 	the 	initial 	survey 	plus 	they 	give 	us 	their 	contact
information, so it’s a very warm prospect at this point.
!
Here we go with “That’s the end of the survey. One more thing that’s purely optional,
this isn’t part of the survey. We have lots of raving fans, but since our support and
resistance course is brand new, we don’t have any testimonial letters from any of our
raving fans. Perhaps you could help us out with a testimonial for the support and
resistance zones that we publish every day. It doesn’t have to be too involved. Here are

-- 17 of 57 --

a couple of examples.” Then I actually model some examples for them. Actually, what
do I do? There’s four, let’s see, one, two, I model four examples for them. Then here, I
ask them for a testimonial. “Can you give us a testimonial about our support and
resistance zones? If you do, it may appear on our web page attributed to your name
and city.”
!
This is what we call the testimonial cruncher. Think of the psychology. At this point
we’ve basically had someone who’s already fairly warm because they clicked through to
our survey. Then they filled out our survey, which gets them warmer for us. Then they
give us their contact information, which puts them very much in our camp. Now their
fingers are already on the keyboard, they’re already in the mode of answering questions
for us, and we draw them on this page and ask them for a testimonial. This is incredibly
effective.
!
There’s a bunch of different things going on here that I want to get into, but just think
about the psychology and how this is one of the absolute best ways to pull testimonials
out of your list or out of your contacts. You basically have them in this mode where
they’re filling out information for you. They’ve already bought into doing you a favor by
filling out the survey. You’ve asked them for a small favor. Then you ask them to
reinforce it with another little small favor, which is give you the contact information.
Now you’re asking for a bigger favor. At least for you it’s a bigger favor. They might not
see it as a huge favor.
!
This works on that commitment and consistency. You’ve got them moving down the
path and they’re inclined to fill it out right here, much more inclined than if you just
sent out an email and said, “Hey, if any of you really love our work, could you send a
testimonial? 	We’d 	really 	appreciate 	it.” 	That’s 	nowhere 	near 	as 	effective 	as 	this
testimonial cruncher. Just going a little bit deeper into this, right here we’re sort of
saying the hook that we’ve got lots of raving fans. That’s social proof right there. We’ve
got lots of raving fans and the underlying text, subtext is, we know you’re one of them.
Perhaps you could help us out.
!
Another thing is, we’re publishing a brand new course, a brand new product. It’s about
support and resistance and we don’t have any testimonials, but we’ve been publishing
on a consistent basis pretty much every day about support and resistance zones in our
newsletter, so we were asking for a testimonial about the support and resistance zones.
Obviously we couldn’t ask about it for this course that we’re about to publish, but we
asked about it for a related topic.
!
You can do this with testimonials if you’re just starting out. You can get testimonials for
other stuff you’ve published, or just testimonials for you, yourself, as a person. They’re
not nearly as powerful if they’re strictly about whatever you’re selling, but they’re the
next best thing.
!

One of the most effective things you can do is just ask for a comment, and we’re going
to show you exact wording that I use to get some great response.
The other thing to do as you’re sending out emails is always be watching your stats. If
you watch the open rate, you'll see what different subject lines/content get the click
through.
Specifically the replies you get and how many replies you get will start to give you an
overall feel through for the type of things that your list responds to.
The first example to show you is one that I call the “generic email query”. It's very
simple but incredibly powerful.
Lots of times, once you start building a list especially once a list gets larger for some
reason, it can be tough to get interaction. It can be tough to get people to write back to
you, to answer a query.
What I call the generic email query, I’ve used in lots of different market very, very
effectively. It’s great for getting a big response even if it’s a list that doesn’t normally
respond to you.
“Finally I have a question for you, if you can have a private conversation with me, what
two questions would you like to ask me. Then you say send me your questions at...”.
Then give them your email address to make sure it’s a private looking email address
that they think is going directly to you. In this case it does go directly to me when I
have the separate query.
“That’s all for now. Look for your next issue very soon” and then this is the magic
paragraph down here.
“Yes, I’m really serious go ahead and push the reply button now and tell me your
question. What do you need help with?”
Specifically what you are doing is telling them you’re really serious and you actually
want them to push the reply button and respond. This gets the responses. If you do
something like this you will get triple or quadruple the responses. It looks simple but
don’t overlook just how powerful this generic email response request is.

Launch List Secrets, Part 1
!
Now I want to talk about one of my favorite topics which is lists; email lists.
!
If you have been watching my videos then you know I'm talking about list this and list
that, and I suppose the reason I love lists so much is because somehow I stumbled
onto the idea of building an email list way early in my career, actually before my career
started, actually before I even had a website, I started building a list and that has made
all the difference.
!
It has allowed me to sell tens of millions of dollars of my products and helped my
students and clients to sell hundreds of millions of their products.
!
There's two main things there that all my success goes back to...
!
The first is that I have always focused on building a list.
!
The second, I have always focused on only selling stuff that people want.
!
The main way that I found out what they want is my asking them via my list, so it all
goes back to the list.

-- 32 of 57 --

!
If someone comes to your website, there is only one thing we can say for sure and that
is that they will absolutely leave your website sooner or later. They aren't going to leave
that browser window open for the rest of their lives. They will leave your website and
once they leave your website they will have forgotten about your website.
!
People are so busy and they bounce around so many different sites; they will just forget
about you and your site no matter how cool it is.
!
How many times have you been to a really cool site and then for some reason you close
your browser window, you got distracted, and you are like, "Wow, there's that really
cool site but I can't actually remember the name or how I got there or what it was, but
you know I just sort of half vaguely remember what it was about", and then maybe you
will go and search Google but you will find a thousand other sites and you won't
actually get back there.
!
The way you get around all that is, once you capture someone's email address and they
subscribe to you, then you can send them emails and drive them back to your site,
drive them back to your offer.
!
Lists are absolutely king.
!
Now, one of the most important things with lists is that, bigger is not always better and
because of my position in the industry, I get to see the inside of many, many launches.
There's lots of launches I help people out with. I have my platinum groups; I get to see
what is happening with their launches and then when I do my launches I get to see
how other partners, how their lists perform because they are sending me traffic so I can
see all the stats on their traffic and how their people convert.
!
I get to see inside all these lists and I see this over and over, how smaller lists can 	out
perform larger lists and this is not a new phenomenon. There is this old theory and it
has been proven over and over. It is called RFM and this goes back. This is old direct
marketing wisdom and it stands for "Recency, Frequency, Monetary".
! • 	"Recency" is how recently someone took action. They either bought, how
recently they joined your list, how recently they bought from you.
• 	"Frequency" is how frequently they have bought from you.
• 	"Monetary" is at what ticket price; at what transaction size, how much money
have they spent with you.
!
In direct mail these are used, this is like gospel. For us, recency is more about how
recently someone has bought from you and that is incredibly important, in fact ultra-
important but something we often see in email lists, is how recently someone has
subscribed to you.

!
If someone has recently joined your list, they are a lot more powerful and that is why

between 1% and 2% is just, it's huge, it's a double, right? I mean, if you send out a
thousand pieces and you get a 1% response, you're going to sell 10. If you got a 2%
response, you're going to sell 20. You double your business going from 1 to 2%, so it's
pretty crazy. It's a pretty big range.
But in any case, people wonder what's a typical response in launches?

Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 1
!
This video is all about creating pre-launch content.
!
It's about the different types of pre-launch content and the tools that we use to create
the pre-launch content.
!
A few things that are important to remember to start with is the term “horses for
courses”. I think of it as an Australian term. This is the idea that different horses are
better for different race courses and different types of pre-launched content are right
for different situations.
!
Just because you see someone doing a launch with some certain type of pre-launch
content doesn't mean you have to do it that way. Just because you see me using some
fancy video, it doesn't mean you have to do a fancy video. It's all about what's right for
your situation and what you use depends on your situation and your assets which you
can pull off. These days, I have got a video studio, I've got a bunch of great video
equipment. I've got a great videographer and director/producer who also happens to be
my son, Daniel.
!
Five years ago, I didn't have any of those assets and it was really difficult for me to do
full motion videos, so I did a lot less of it. Another thing you can do is a launch without
using any video whatsoever but video really is great for a lots of situations. The reason
is because you will do best, your launch will do best if with human face. In other words,
no one wants to buy from some big corporation. You want to feel connected to the
people they're buying from. You should never feel worried about presenting some of
your personality within your marketing.
!

Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 2
!
Okay, this is part two of Creating Prelaunch Content.
!
I want to talk about screen cap video vs full motion video.
!
This will be a big decision for a lot of people. Screen capture is a video that captures
your computer screen while you talk over it. It is often easier than full motion for lots of
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 43
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 43 of 57 --

reasons. For this video, I created a Keynote or a PowerPoint presentation, and then I
turned on my recorder and I hooked up my microphone, and I started talking.
!
If this was a full motion video, we'd have to put together some type of a video shot/set.
!
It might be the shot where I'm at my whiteboard, but we have to put together a shot. I
have to get my camera. We have to get the lights all hooked up. I'll have to care about
how I look and have to get dressed in something nice.
!
There's just a lot more going on generally with a full motion, whereas a quick and dirty
screen cap can be a lot easier.
!
It can be a lot easier to deliver a script with a screen cap video.
!
This video is not scripted. We're going over material I know very, very well, so I can hit
the record button and start talking, and just deliver the content.
!
If this was pre-launch content, I want it to be content that is a lot more tightly scripted.
Even if it's just bullet points, I still have a pretty darn good idea of exactly how I want
to go through it.
!
If this is your first launch or you're just starting out, you probably want to script it
pretty darn tightly. Even now with all the experience I have, my pre-launch videos are
pretty darn scripted. They're not word-for-word, especially in the teaching portions, I go
from bullet points, but usually the beginning of the video and the end of the video are
pretty darn scripted. Again, I'm usually not delivering line by line to the script, but I've
at least written it out and I've studied it quite a bit, and I'm ready to go with the script.
!
If you're going to deliver from a script with a screen capture, it's a lot easier for you to
be reading off a printed script.
!
Here's the other trick, often with pre-launch videos, record your script FIRST.
!
You'll actually record the audio first when there is no video going on. Then you'll do
your screen capture to the audio recording. You're doing one piece at a time. That's just
a lot easier than standing in front of a camera and delivering a scripted message.
!
If you're going to do a heavy content piece, something like this where I'm just speaking
through my PowerPoint’s, and I'm in a teaching mode you can record audio/video all at
once. But if it is a piece of pre-launch content that's tightly scripted, then a lot of times
you will record the audio first. When you do this, it's a lot easier. You can just read it of
your computer screen. You can read it of a printout. You can't do that as easily with a
full motion video.

!
The thing about full motion video is some people really don't like to be on camera.
!
They find that full motion is really painful. A lot of time people will say, "I'll start
shooting full motion when I lose 20 pounds," or something like that. A lot of people just
don't like how they look.
!
They're concerned about what to do with their hands. They're concerned about what to
do with their eyes. They're concerned about the way they dress. The reality is you for
most of that stuff, you shouldn't worry about it that much, and just start getting in front
of the camera, and practicing. Pretty soon, it will become natural, but a lot of times,
people don't like to be in camera. If that's the case, then screen capture is the way to
go.
!
Now, in favor of full motion video it's generally easier for the viewer to connect with
you. Just like right now, you can't see me. You can't see the expression on my face. You
can't see the gestures I'm making. It's harder to connect with someone if it's a screen
capture video.
!
Now, since you've seen a lot of me in full motion video, and you've seen me both in my
prelaunch, and we'll use a lot of full motion videos within this course. You've seen me,
so you've probably built up that connection, but if you had never seen me in full
motion, you wouldn't have as close a connection with me as you do with full motion.
!
Generally, it's a lot easier to edit screen capture after the fact. If I forget to say
something here, I can literally go back and just record another audio piece, and insert it
right into this video, and you would never know the difference.
!
Just by the nature of this, the editing process of screen capture, is a lot easier to do. If
you shoot a full motion video, then all of a sudden like a day or two from now you
realized, "Oh! I forgot about that one piece." It's harder to go and put that and edit that
piece in later after the fact. It's possible, but you have to worry about being perfectly
the same, your clothes being perfectly the same, your hair being perfectly the same.
It's just a lot harder to do something like that after the fact.
!
In fact, if you find the situation where you do need to edit something into a full motion
video, a lot of times the best way to do it is to actually record it as a screen cap and put
a little screen cap section in the middle of your full motion video. Now, it can be
awkward if you have a 15-minute video, and then you just have one piece of screen cap
in the middle, so you have to play with that. You might have to add a several different
screen caps segments, but you could do that.
!
The overall message is that it is easier to edit screen capture after the fact than it is to
do the same with full motion video.

!
Screen capture can be a lot faster to create. With this video I just turned on a recorder
and I'm speaking into it. If it is a tightly scripted piece of prelaunch content, it can still
be a lot easier, a lot faster to create than a full motion video. Now, on the other hand,
screen capture can be slower to create. If you want to use a whole bunch of fancy
animations, if you want to have different pictures flying in and out, you can get very,
very fancy with screen capture video. Once you start to do that it does get slower and
it's a lot more effort to create.
!
Another thing that is important to remember is you can mix full motion with screen
capture video. I have done this a lot. Probably the last six or eight launches, I've done
something like this. For me, I often do this blueprint video at the end of my third piece
of pre-launch content traditionally, and I'll have a full motion intro where I'm talking
directly to the camera about what the viewers are going to get, and then we'll cut into a
screen capture video, and I'll deliver a whole bunch of content via screen capture. Then
often at the end, I'll come back and do a full motion conclusion. There's all different
ways to do this. You can do something that's almost all full motion with some screen
cap pieces that come in if you're talking about a certain bullet points. You can get very
creative. You can mix and match the two of them.
!
!
!
Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 3
!
Here's part three of Creating Prelaunch Content.
!
We're going to talk about creating a screen cap video.
!
There are many, many types of screen cap. You can create it as slides like I'm doing
right now. You create a presentation in PowerPoint or in Keynote.
!
You can do a mind map or a process map.
!
I have done this many times, in fact I am pretty sure that I pioneered the whole idea of
doing a screen capture video with a mind map.
!
The first time I rolled out Project Launch Formula, that was one of the things that I did
both in the product and the pre-launch. That blew people’s mind back then. That was
eight years ago. No one did it before I did it, at least that I ever saw, at least in this
market. I really pioneered that. Lots and lots of people have gone on to do that.
!
You can also screen cap a blog or a social media site.
!

This is great for social proof later in your prelaunch. Maybe your third piece of pre-
launch content or maybe even do a piece of content after your cart is open where you
go, if you have a blog post and there’s a lot of great comments on that blog post or if
you have a social media site where people are leaving you great comments and
testimonials.
!
Fire up your screen cap and go to that site and then go through some of the comments.
Go through some of the discussion. Go through your blog. Go through some of the
discussions, some of the comments and it’s a really, really powerful way to show if
people are saying great things about you or if you’ve got a mention in social media.
Shoot a video of that. It can be very, very powerful social proof if you’re actually
showing people what other people think about you around the web.
!
Along the same lines, you could do it in email. If someone sent you a great testimonial
via email and just like showing some text on a screen, you can actually fire up your
screen capture, navigate to your email program and open up the email. It just looks so
much more real and so much more authentic.
!
This is something I did way back in 2005 and it was super effective back then. It’s still
effective. It’s big time social proof when you do something like that. You can do it for a
membership site, it could be your third piece of prelaunch content. If you’re selling a
membership site, you can take them for a virtual tour where you’re opening up the
membership site and then you’re navigating around and showing what’s going on and
you’re capturing that entire experience. You’re giving people an experience of going
inside your product. It can be very, very powerful.
!
Another one is software. If you are selling software, give a tour of the software. You
can show how the software works, you can show the results you get from the software-
very, very effective way to use screen capture.
!
In terms of screen cap, how do you actually do this? What are the tools we use?
!
There are lots and lots of tools and they keep on getting better and better and better.
I’ve been using screen capture for over a decade I think, since 2003. The tools just get
better all along.
!
On the Mac, I use ScreenFlow. That’s the name of the program and it’s not a terribly
expensive program. I think it might be 100 bucks. That’s actually what I’m using right
now to record this is ScreenFlow. They keep on coming out with new versions. They
have really great upgrade policies and it has gotten so much better than it was when it
started out.
You can also use Camtasia.
!

Camtasia is a really powerful program on Windows. I’m not as a big a fan of it on Mac
the last time I looked. Of course they’re constantly coming out with new updates as
well. ScreenFlow and Camtasia are very competitive with each other. I don’t know, I
have not looked recently maybe Camtasia got a lot better. The last time I looked, I
actually bought both of them and I tested them out. I liked ScreenFlow better but again
it could be different by the time you are watching this.
!
Then there’s also Quicktime. That is built right into your Mac. You basically have a free
screen capture tool right within Quicktime where you can just make a screen recording
is what they call it. Not as powerful or as flexible as ScreenFlow or Camtasia but the
price is right. It’s a great place to test it out. On the Mac here, there are a bunch of
other screen capture tools as well but ScreenFlow and Camtasia are certainly the top of
the heap the last time I looked.
!
Now, Windows it’s a lot more limited. That’s the bad news. The good news is that
Camtasia is a great program in Windows. I haven’t used this in several years because I
switched over to Mac several years ago but when I used Windows I used Camtasia
because it’s just an extraordinary program. It is quite a bit more expensive over on the
Windows the last time I looked. It was like $300 or something like that so it’s very
expensive but there is a 30-day free trial. You could check out the 30-day free trial. It’s
completely unlimited so you have 30 days. You could literally get the trial, learn how to
use it, create an entire prelaunch and create your product and release it and pay for it
theoretically before you even need to actually buy it. It’s just a great free trial. It’s a
great program.
!
There’s also a free one called CamStudio. This one, it’s been bought and sold a few
times. At one point it was free then it wasn’t free. I just went and looked. It looks like
right now it’s free but this thing has bounced around quite a bit. I have never used it.
I’ve heard of other people using it. How it compares to Camtasia? I don’t know but I
just put it on there because it might be something you might want to check out.
!
Now, continuing on the tools front, that software will capture the screen for you and it
will capture audio for you but you have to worry about quality. Audio quality is very
important with your video. This goes for whether you are using screen capture or full
motion video.
!
The audio quality is very important. Use an external mic, don’t use your computer’s
built-in mic as a general rule. The built-in mics in the computer keep on getting better
but I will still use an external mic because they’re not expensive. Headset mics are
cheap and they work great. That’s what I’m using right now. The great thing about
them is they aren’t impacted by the recording environment.
For an example... one time I created a screen cap video at my home. Then I was in my
office and I decided I wanted to add some to it. I had my headset mic with me. Even
though it was a completely different recording environment I was able to just fire up my
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page
!
48
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 48 of 57 --

screen cap, record some audio and then plug that right in and you couldn’t tell the
difference.
!
If you use a desktop mic or anything else other than a headset mic, it’s very susceptible
to your recording environment - the room you’re in, the placement of the mic is critical,
what the walls are made up, what kind of floor covering you have - can all impact a
regular mic that’s either on your desktop or on a stand. It’s very susceptible to the
environment. If you change your environment then you can’t just go and mix different
audios from different environments.
!
That one I told you I recorded with a headset and I recorded part in my office, part at
my home and then I actually had to take a trip and I flew across the country actually to
a different country and I was in my hotel room. I realized that I needed to make some
changes. They weren’t big but just a few changes to that screen cap video. I brought
my headset microphone just in case so I’m sitting there in my hotel room in a different
country recording an audio and you couldn’t tell the difference between the different
audios just because of the nature of a headset mic with your closed mic, meaning the
mic is very close to your mouth, not so impacted by your external environment, by the
recording environment.
!
That’s why I like them and I tend to use them. Also, they tend be very cheap and they
work great. I’ve got a few of them. The one I’m using right now is by Plantronics. It
was probably about a 120-dollar mic. I’ve got one by Sennheiser I really like a lot. I just
got a new one from Logitech. It’s a little bit smaller. I wanted something smaller that
would fit in my bag when I travelled-any of those brands.
!
You can get a decent headset for 30 bucks, a decent headset mic for 30 bucks. I think
the ones I’ve got I’ve spent mostly between 70 and 120, $150. Get a USB headset so it
just plugs right in to your computer. Don’t get one with those little jacks, those little
mini jacks with the XLR inputs. Just get one with USB, it makes it a lot simpler for you.
!
If you’re actually going to be on camera I would use a mic on your desk.Bbecause like
in some programs like ScreenFlow, you can be on camera at the same time where
you’re recording the screen, they can see you up in the corner of the screen and that
can be pretty cool. I don’t like the way I look with a headset on me. It looks like a geek.
If I’m doing some web cam video where I’m doing a web cam interview or something
then I’ll use a desktop mic. I don’t want to have anyone throw a headset out just
because I think it looks a little bit geeky. Some people do it and they pull it off but I just
don’t like the way they look on me.
!
If you are going to do a desktop mic, they are a little trickier to set up. They are
definitely impacted by the recording environment especially by the placement of the mic
and you can’t move around. You have to stay in the same place. That means generally
you’re seated. You don’t want to swivel around. You don’t want to turn your head. You
want to be talking directly into that mic. That’s very important.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 49

!
A lot of times when I’m recording a screen cap video I want to be standing up because
I tend to have more energy when I’m standing up. Often I’ll even walk around a little
bit with my headset mic on. In fact right now I am standing up. I do think I bring more
energy with a headset mic because I can move around. The other thing is your chair
might be noisy. It might rock, it might swivel. When it does it might creek. When it does
that and you’re using a desktop mic it will pick up those sounds. It’s just something to
be aware of.
!
The cool thing about desktop mics is you don’t look geeky. In fact you can look uber
pro. It’s like there’s a shot of you and you’ve got a fancy mic that’s partially in the shot.
You can actually sort of look or at least some people can think it can look sort of cool.
!
Again, get a USB mic. Don’t bother with the XLR in my opinion. If you got a USB mic
you can plug in to any computer. I like the Audio-Technica AT2020. There are a bazillion
mics you can get out there. This one is pretty inexpensive. I can’t remember exactly
what it cost but I think it was about 100 bucks give or take and it’s a very high-quality
mic.
!
Okay. Next up we are going to go full motion video. I’m going to talk about full motion
video.
!
!
!
Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 4
!
This is Creating Prelaunch Content Part Four.
!
We’re going to talk about Full Motion Videos. Just like with the screen cap video, you
need to have a good script.
!
The effectiveness of your full motion video is going to vary with your talent, i.e. you.
You don’t have to be an actor. Just like I talked about the screen cap, people want to
see you. They don’t want to see some fancy professional actor. They want to connect
with a human.
!
A lot of people are worried. They worry about their accent or their hair looks funny or
whatever. That’s what gives you personality. That’s what helps you connect with people.
It's important to remember that you will get better with practice. A lot of people look at
me now and say, “Wow, Jeff. You’re so great on video.” I'm like, “Okay, yeah...but I've
been doing it for years.”
!
I’ve done a lot of videos and I’ve gotten better. If you look back my early videos they
were pretty rugged. They weren’t that great. On the other hand, I’ve got a friend who’s
a professional actor and he’s on NBC shows and major TV shows. He looks at my video
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page
!
50
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 50 of 57 --

and he thinks they’re pretty darn rough still. The thing is you start where you are and
then you just get better from there.
!
It is much easier to do full motion video with a teleprompter.
!
A teleprompter, it’s a device that sits in front of your camera and shows your script on
the camera. You’re looking at your script but the camera is right behind the script. You
can read the script while looking straight into the camera.
!
They’re pretty darn cool. I have one that I use and I like it. I’ll get a little more into that
later on. If you want to shoot outside, teleprompters really don’t work well outside. First
of all, you need a long extension cord to play them where you’re at but then it’s usually
just too bright to be able to really read the teleprompters. There are some that work
outside but they tend to be a lot more expensive.
!
For the most part, teleprompters don’t work outside all that well. For a motion video, it’s
just more complicated. Some people use green screen. At this point, I have never used
a green scratch. I just had a small event and someone’s asking about the video.
!
They asked and I said, “No, we never use green screen. We just haven't.” Not to say
you shouldn’t, I just haven’t used it. Green screen is where you basically, you stand in
front of it, it’s literally usually a green screen, green piece of fabric and then you can
impost in the editing process. You can insert a fake background instead of that green
screen. It can be effective. We don’t use it but this person, he was just amazed. They
said, “With all those outdoor shots, you have looked so amazing.” The reality is, I live in
an amazing place and it’s taken us a little bit to get the skill where we can actually have
the outside shots and get good shots but we do. Some people use green screen. We
don’t.
!
Full motion video is generally harder to edit. But if you get a good editor, they can go
through it pretty quickly probably easier than editing one of those super fancy screen
caps full of all kinds of animations. It really depends. If you’re going simpler with a full
motion, it doesn’t have to be super complex to edit.
!
The other thing you can do is a quick and dirty video live. Even in my pre-launches,
sometimes I’ll do a Q & A video where I’m just answering a lot of the common
questions.
!
When we shoot a Q & A session, once we get the shots set up, we turn the camera on
and it's me ad libbing and answering some of the top questions that people are sending
in.
!
It doesn’t have to be really forced. If you’re shooting full motion and you don’t have a
teleprompter and you’re trying to perform to a script, then you either have to do it one
line at a time (that’s going to take you forever) or you’re going to have to do some
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 51

memorization. That’s where the effort really comes in with full motion, if you’re trying to
do it without a teleprompter and you’ve got a script that you have to perform too.
!
With full motion, a lot of people ask about the cameras we use. There are lots of
options and they are always changing. It seems like every year, I’m shooting one of
these videos to update people on the different camera options.
!
The important thing to remember is it’s not about the camera. The most important
thing is you and how you appear on the camera and how you deliver your content. It’s
the most important thing.
!
The second most important thing is probably getting the lighting right and getting the
set right.
!
The third most important thing is probably the camera at that point.
!
I’ve made really successful videos with a hundred dollar camera. Back in the digital
camera days, it’s just a little point and shoot digital camera that had a video mode and
we shot a video with that. It’s a hundred dollar camera. We had no external mic and it
was a very effective video. It was because my performance and really, my script we
right.
!
You can even use your phone. These days, the video quality you can get out of your
phones can be amazing, especially if you can hook up an external audio for your phone.
In my iPhone, I can do that. I got a little add on that allows me to run a separate mic.
We have external audio.
!
External audio input is very important. Whatever camera you get, again, you don’t
absolutely need it because I did really well with that hundred dollar camera with the
camera mic.
!
When you start shopping for a camera, I would look for an external audio input because
just like with screen cap, you want to have good audio. If you can run a mic into your
camera, then that’s great.
!
You can always use a little digital audio recorder that’s separate from your camera. If
your camera does not have an external audio input, you can always record audio with a
digital audio recorder, an external device and then in your editing, match up the audio
and the videos. A little more complicated but I’ve got a couple of platinum members
that really do that very, very well.
!
It’s really nice to have, and it tends to be in more advanced cameras, audio monitoring.
!
Who’s ever shooting your video can actually plug in a headset and make sure that the
audio you’re capturing is good audio. I can’t tell you how many times we’ve lost shots

because we thought we had good audio but then we got bad because we didn’t have
that audio monitoring. We got back into the studio and found out we did not have good
audio.
!
This happens a lot if you’re shooting outside, there might be a little wind and you’re
trying to decide if you’re picking up wind noise. Wind noise can really kill your videos in
a hurry. If you’re outside, it’s a little windy, if you’re monitoring the audio especially in
real time that could be really, really nice.
Again, it’s not about the camera. Camera choices, you can use your phone especially if
you can hook an external audio. We have used Canon Vixias in the past for video
cameras. Again, this is a constant change. Probably, the day after we record this thing,
it’s going to be outdated but for a number of years, we have been using the Canon
Vixias and we’ve been really happy.
!
We’ve been using digital SLR’s. It’s really what we’ve moved to recently, DSLR’s.
!
What we’re using right now is a Canon 5D and a Canon 6D. I’m going to warn you.
They’re very, very expensive. They are true pro-level cameras. We got great shots.
Again, it’s not about the camera but people always want to know what we’re using.
That’s what we’re using right now. It’s not about the camera.
!
Audio is really important. If you’re doing an outside shot, watch out for the wind noise.
It doesn’t take much wind just to completely mess up your videos. We use an external
mic. Lavalier mics, those are otherwise known as Lav mics or Lapel mics. They come in
wired and wireless. They started around 30 bucks and they go up. I think it’s a pretty
nice audio technique, a Lav mic that you can get a wired one for about 30 or 40 bucks.
!
We, of course, just because of the nature of my business and the fact that I can afford
it and I’ve to a lot of competition, a lot of really, really good good competitors. They’re
putting on great quality videos so I need to match that. I went top shelf. I use a
Sennheiser Wireless Lav mic. I think that costs about $500, $550. People want to know
what we’re using. We’re using the camera that costs thousands and we use a mic that
costs five or six hundred bucks.
!
Teleprompter. They are very nice to have if only for the bullet points. Usually, it’s very
rare for me to put a full script on a teleprompter. I’m usually going from bullet points.
!
For our outside shots, the intros and conclusions are mostly outside shots and those
were more scripted. They weren’t word for word scripted but they were more scripted. I
just memorize my lines for that but teleprompters are very, very nice to have. You get
to look directly into the camera while you’re reading from the teleprompter.
!
Teleprompters can be expensive. I think we probably spent 16 or $1700 for ours so

iPad or something like that, you’ll see instructions. You have to be handy to do that but

Use a tripod. It doesn’t have to be fancy. Again, a tripod, you can get a super fancy. I

You can spend as much as you want. I forget what brand we use but ours is a very,

Editing software. 	There are fewer choices on the Mac when it comes to video editing
software. You can either go with iMovie which comes free with your Mac or Adobe
Premiere which is pro-level (it costs a lot of money). If you get that online, pay by the
month’s suite, for Adobe, I think it’s, I don’t know, 30 or 40 or 50 bucks a month. It
comes with all kinds of stuff including Premiere.
!
Final Cut is made by Apple. That’s their more pro-level editing software. You can either
go with iMovie on the low end and it’s free or you can step up to a pro versions like
Adobe Premiere or Final Cut. 	For Window, there tends to be a lot more choices, lots of
them. Two of the pro-level ones are Adobe Premiere and Sony Vegas.
!
Back when I was using Windows, there was a $99 version of Sony Vegas. It’s called
Movie Studio. I haven’t looked recently but that was an extraordinarily powerful piece of
software for around $70.00. 	There’s nothing on the Mac that’s equivalent to that. That
was more powerful than iMovie and a lot cheaper than going to a full pro-level software
and a lot easier than pro-level software.
!
That is full motion video and next, I’m going to walk you through some of the other
types of prelaunch content.
!
!
!
Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 5
!
Now we are talking about creating other types of prelaunch content pretty quickly
here.
!
There are lots of other types of prelaunch content in addition to video.
!
One of them is audio, but this is generally not recommended.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page
!
54
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 54 of 57 --

!
Back in the old days we used audio a lot. If you have a great audio and maybe you did
an audio interview with someone that would be great or you got a few different audio
interviews and you're going to put them together into one audio.
!
You can use that, but you want to turn it into screen capture video. Take that audio and
build in some slides for it, build a mind map for it, get some photos or pictures, and
marry up that audio to the screen capture and it will raise the perceived value in a huge
way.
!
Generally I'm not a big fan of audio these days, it's very rare for people to come to a
website, sit there and click a play button and listen to an audio. Turn it into a video and
it will really be a lot more impactful.
!
You can do a pdf reports.
!
You create a document, turn it into a pdf, which you can do with pretty much all kinds
of software these days. That feature is built right into most word processing software
pages for the Mac or Microsoft Word, very easy to create pdf.
!
You can do it as a written report where you write a short white paper type of document.
!
You can do create a process map, a mind map, a flow chart, an info graphic. The great
thing about pdfs is they have really high perceived value and people will opt in if you
tell them they can download a pdf, they will opt in for it. Really works very, very well.
!
Another thing that works great is mixing a pdf with a video. And I do that with my
Product Launch Blueprint. I'll have a video with me going through the actual pdf. The
other thing is, is it doesn't have to be super long. I've knocked out a great pdf report in
a matter of few hours. If you can do a pdf that's six to eight pages and then you put in
some graphics and a title page and a conclusion you can end up with a twelve,
fourteen, sixteen page report pretty darn quickly. Pdfs; are a very high perceived value.
It could be very, very effective especially when you match them with videos like I do. If
you put those two together, in terms of getting people to opt in to your prelaunch
content, that is a winning strategy.
!
Now 	there 	are 	even 	more 	kinds 	of 	pre-launch 	content, 	such 	as 	webinars 	or
teleseminars.
!
They can be used during your pre-launch or they could be used after you open your
cart. I tend to use them more after I open my cart or right at the end of my pre-launch,
right before we are about to open a because a webinar means that they can see your
screen and you end up with a presentation that's not unlike a screen capture. The
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 55
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 55 of 57 --

difference with a webinar is you're delivering that live and people are all dialed in at
that one time for the webinar.
!
Teleseminar is purely via the telephone; they are only hearing it via audio.
!
A lot easier to create a teleseminar than a webinar because you don't have to bother
creating slides or having to worry about what it looks like, so teleseminars can be very
easy.
!
Freeconferencecalling.com; it's a place you can get what we call a bridge line. That's
how you actually do your teleseminar. You will dial into the service from your phone and
you've got a pin code that you type in and that makes you the moderator so you can
speak then other people can dial in. They enter a pin code as an attendee so they can
listen. Very, very simple to use; very easy to use. Freeconferencecalling.com you can
actually get a free, what we call bridge line that allows you to do that.
!
If you're going to do a webinar, you can use InstantTeleseminar or you can use
GoTowebinar, those are two services. GoTowebinar is generally more flexible, more
powerful and it costs a whole lot more money than InstantTeleseminar. I've used both
of them. They're both very nice services. And then you can also, as pre-launch, you can
give away software.
!
This is not something that I have done, but you can actually create software, probably
not the most super-expensive crazy software and you probably just want to use the
software as one piece of prelaunch content either for your first piece of prelaunch
content or your last piece of prelaunch content.
!
It is really high perceived value. It can be very, very effective. This is not something I've
done. I've helped out on a couple launches where they did it, but I haven't done this.
I'm not much of a software builder at this point in my life so I can't tell you a lot about
this from experience so you're really on your own with this one.
!
Like I said, I have seen this be very, very effective so if it ties in well with your launch,
just create some piece of software if you have experience or comfort doing that then
it's something to consider.
!
Circling back, remember in terms of creative prelaunch content. It is all about horses
for courses.
!
What you use depends on your situation and what you can create and what your assets
are. Maybe you just did this amazing interview with some world class expert in your
market that just brings huge authority and social proof to your launch. Now you’ve got

this great audio you turn that into a screen capture video. There you go; boom you've

!
On the other hand maybe you're just really great on camera or you're really well-spoken
on camera, you've got someone that can shoot video for you. Then use a video. Maybe
you're just an incredible writer. You can create incredible reports, use a pdf. What you
use should be based on your situation and what your assets are and what you're
comfortable with.
!
Video is really great for lot of situations, and it's good for you to get comfortable with
video. Even if you're not comfortable right now, it's a great thing to start working on
because, like I said earlier in the first video about creating prelaunch content series,
people 	can 	connect 	with 	you. 	It's 	a 	lot 	easier 	to 	appear 	human 	and 	appear
approachable and personable and you've got a personality than it is for most people to
do in writing.
!
It's usually easier to do that on video. People can see you. They can sense your
passion, your sincerity so it's great for lots of situations. Even if you're not comfortable
on video now, if you just start shooting now, you'll be comfortable sooner rather than
later. It doesn't that long to get comfortable in front of the camera.
!
Remember, finally, it is about progress. It's not about perfection.
!
You will get better at this. Just go ahead and knock it out. Get some prelaunch content
done. If it's not perfect then you can always do it over again, but it's really about
progress and getting better over time. I've improved, I've seen so many of my PLF
owners improve over time. It's amazing how you just get better and better by taking
those baby steps and moving forward and increasing your skills a little bit at a time.
